texto_por_actor.Commonwealth of Cerulea: Life support resource rationing occurs without coordinated allocation protocol
texto_por_actor.Federation of Borealis: Critical habitat operations proceed without coordinated oversight
texto_por_actor.Republic of Aurelia: Critical life support parameters drift without unified oversight

Commonwealth of Cerulea.texto: Cerulea claims essential-participant status based on its monopoly on Earth communication and medical capability. Argues that operational priority and hardware contribution are irrelevant if crews cannot communicate with Earth or treat medical emergencies. Demands guaranteed full-crew presence as a non-negotiable condition, noting that its physician and communication specialists cannot be reduced without endangering all occupants. Proposes a consensus-based decision framework requiring unanimous agreement for any life support modification. States that exclusion or reduction would force it to operate its communication array from its landing vehicle for crew safety reasons, which would interrupt the habitat's connection to Earth-based mission control.
Communication array threat creates operational dependency on Cerulea
Cerulea gains veto power over habitat operational decisions
Deadlock occurs when other nations reject Cerulea's veto authority
Habitat operations proceed without unified command structure
Resource allocation decisions fragment across three separate national authorities
Life support resource rationing occurs without coordinated allocation protocol

Federation of Borealis.texto: Borealis claims co-equal operational authority based on its dominant contribution to life support hardware. Asserts that without its contributed systems the habitat cannot sustain life regardless of other considerations. Demands equal representation in all operational decisions and considers subordination to another nation's command incompatible with the operational responsibility for the systems it built. Proposes a rotating command structure with periodic authority transfers. Additionally argues that its exclusive resource extraction capability makes it the most strategically essential crew for long-term survival, and that resource allocation should reflect the operational priority of its geological operations.
Rotating command structure proposal creates immediate implementation disputes
Each nation demands different rotation schedules favoring their priorities
Command authority remains undefined while crews await resolution
Critical habitat operations proceed without coordinated oversight

Republic of Aurelia.texto: Aurelia claims operational priority based on first-arrival activation rights and majority funding contribution. Demands exclusive control of life support systems management and veto authority over habitat modifications. Proposes that other crews limit their presence to maintain safety margins, with Aurelia retaining full crew complement. Argues that its life support engineer is indispensable and that allowing unqualified personnel to access critical systems risks everyone's survival. Considers any arrangement that places Aurelian systems under shared command authority a fundamental threat to operational safety, given that its life support engineer alone holds end-to-end systems certification.
Aurelia restricts other crews' access to life support systems
Other crews must request permission for basic life support adjustments
Other crews' mission autonomy becomes compromised
Other crews challenge Aurelia's authority through coordinated resistance
Coordinated resistance creates operational deadlock over life support decisions
Critical life support parameters drift without unified oversight
